summary: Matched and cleaned up several Peach float and side-special functions after compiler/toolchain improvements made prior inline-assembly fallbacks unnecessary. The PR removed large MUST_MATCH asm blocks from ftPe_Float.c, ftPe_FloatAttack.c, and ftPe_SpecialS.c, exposed shared float helpers through headers, moved updateFloatDir into ftPe_FloatFall.c, and used small C-level type/constant/stack-shaping changes to preserve matching.
what_changed: Removed inline assembly fallback blocks for matched Peach float functions including ftPe_8011BAD8 and ftPe_Float_Anim, leaving the C implementations active.
Removed asm fallbacks in ftPe_FloatAttack.c for ftPe_8011BE80 and ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA.
Removed many asm fallbacks in ftPe_SpecialS.c around side-special start, air-start, air-jump, enter-start, enter-air-start, and enter-air-end-smash paths.

Deleted local float constant labels such as ftPe_Float_804D97C0, ftPe_Float_804D97C4, lbl_804D97C8, lbl_804D97CC, and the MUST_MATCH-only double lbl_804D97F0, replacing uses with direct literals such as 0.0f and 1.0f where shown.
Changed ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput from a commented-static declaration to a normal external helper and reflected that in ftPe_Float.h.
Moved getFloatDir/updateFloatDir logic out of ftPe_Float.c into ftPe_FloatFall.c and added updateFloatDir to ftPe_FloatFall.h.
Adjusted ftPe_SpecialSStart_Coll to take Fighter_GObj* and retrieve the fighter with gobj->user_data instead of GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Adjusted some facing_dir comparisons to explicit floating constants, notably (float) -1.0 and 1.0f.
In ftPe_SpecialS.c, folded the former setCallbacks helper into enterAirJump and added an explicit unused-stack padding block under MUST_MATCH.
Split out doPostEnd_SmallerStack with explicit unused stack padding and switched enterAirEndSmash to call it instead of doPostEnd.
smart_moves: Used the newer compiler behavior to retire hundreds of lines of hand-written inline assembly while keeping the existing C logic as the source of truth.
Exported ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput rather than keeping it pseudo-static because ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA calls it across translation-unit boundaries.
Moved updateFloatDir to ftPe_FloatFall.c, closer to the FloatFall motion-state logic it selects, and exposed only the needed declaration in ftPe_FloatFall.h.
Replaced label-backed float constants with direct float literals in places where the compiler now emitted matching code, reducing unnecessary rodata symbols.
Used precise type nudges, such as Fighter_GObj* and direct gobj->user_data access, as matching tools rather than broad rewrites.
Preserved stack-sensitive matching with small explicit unused arrays under MUST_MATCH instead of reverting whole functions to assembly.
Created a smaller-stack variant of post-end logic instead of forcing one helper to satisfy multiple stack layouts.
decomp_lessons: After compiler upgrades, revisit WIP/MUST_MATCH asm functions: previously necessary asm may now be replaceable by existing C with minimal edits.
When a helper is used by another Peach module, make linkage honest in both the C file and header instead of leaving a commented-out static marker.
Relocating helpers can be a matching tactic: moving updateFloatDir from Float to FloatFall removed local static/rodata complications while keeping behavior readable.
Direct literals can sometimes match better than named const-label symbols after toolchain changes; verify generated rodata before preserving artificial labels.
For stack-sensitive functions, targeted unused-stack padding can be less harmful than retaining large inline asm blocks.
Function signature specificity, such as Fighter_GObj* versus HSD_GObj*, can affect codegen and data access patterns enough to help matching.
If one logical helper needs two stack profiles, introducing a narrowly named variant like doPostEnd_SmallerStack may be preferable to contorting a single implementation.
naming_conventions: Peach files continue using ftPe_ prefixes for character-specific functions.
Motion-state enum names use ftPe_MS_* forms such as ftPe_MS_FloatFallF, ftPe_MS_FloatFallB, ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSJump, and ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSEnd_0.
Data attribute type is ftPe_DatAttrs and local variables commonly use da for dat_attrs.
Shared helper names in this slice include ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput and updateFloatDir.
The new helper name doPostEnd_SmallerStack explicitly documents that its purpose is stack-shape-sensitive matching rather than a gameplay distinction.
Existing unknown-address names such as ftPe_8011BAD8, ftPe_8011BE80, and ftPe_8011BF34 were retained when no semantic rename was provided.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) asm wrappers once the C versions matched.
Changed asm comments for remaining ftPe_8011BB6C loads from symbol names to direct 0.0 and 1.0 literals, matching the removed constant labels.
Used direct floating comparisons for facing_dir, including explicit casts like (float) -1.0, to influence emitted comparison code.
Used Fighter_GObj* plus gobj->user_data in ftPe_SpecialSStart_Coll to alter generated access sequences.
Added explicit unused stack arrays under MUST_MATCH, including u8 _[8] in enterAirJump and u8 _[34] = { 0 } in doPostEnd_SmallerStack.
Inlined callback assignment into enterAirJump after removing setCallbacks, likely to match call/stack/codegen layout.
Split doPostEnd_SmallerStack from doPostEnd to satisfy a smaller stack requirement in enterAirEndSmash without disturbing other callers.
Moved updateFloatDir into ftPe_FloatFall.c and changed constants from lbl_804D97C8/lbl_804D97CC to 1.0f/0.0f in the Fighter_ChangeMotionState call.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states: "Addressed some functions that were fixed by new compiler" and "Fixed a few functions that needed work."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftPe_8011BAD8, ftPe_8011BE80, or ftPe_8011BF34 semantically.
Check whether updateFloatDir remains globally exposed or is later made static again after file organization changes.
Search for other PRs after the compiler update that remove MUST_MATCH asm blocks via literal constants or unused-stack arrays.
Investigate whether doPostEnd_SmallerStack survived long-term or was folded back after more struct/codegen improvements.
Compare ftPe_SpecialS collision functions in later revisions for more complete typing of Fighter_GObj versus HSD_GObj.
